response.justification: The candidate has extensive software development experience but lacks direct C++ development on the Windows platform in recent roles, which is a mandatory requirement. While he has C++ experience from earlier roles (National Instruments, 2014-2015), it is outdated and not aligned with capital markets or fixed-income systems. Domain expertise in financial services is missing, and optional skills like Summit or STL are not evidenced.
strengths: Strong software development background with experience in multiple languages and frameworks (C++, D, JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js)
Proven ability to work in high-performance, low-latency environments (e.g., real-time metadata handling at Dunnhumby)
Experience with CI/CD pipelines, testing practices (TDD, BDD), and DevOps tooling
concerns: No demonstrated C++ development on Windows in the last 9 years (mandatory skill)
Lacks capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income domain experience
No evidence of SQL Server/Oracle proficiency or Summit platform experience
